commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a macOS node-mode TLS session cache keyed by URL and TLS parameters, routes direct `wss://` session construction through it, and adds Swift regression tests for reuse and rebuild behavior.

PR surface: Other +78. Total +78 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction was run here. Source inspection gives a clear path: current main rebuilds the macOS TLS session object on each node loop, while `GatewayNodeSession` treats changed session identity as reconnect input.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor or maintainer macOS runtime proof before merge, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix #90668: [Bug]: macOS node mode can silently self-reconnect in a healthy direct gateway session
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #90668.
- Keeps macOS node-mode direct `wss://` gateway sessions from rebuilding the TLS-pinning WebSocket session on every healthy run-loop pass.
- Adds focused Swift regression coverage for unchanged TLS inputs reusing the same session object and changed TLS inputs rebuilding it.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `MacNodeModeCoordinator.run()` called `buildSessionBox(...)` on every loop pass, and `buildSessionBox` always created a fresh `GatewayTLSPinningSession` / `WebSocketSessionBox` for `wss://` URLs.
- Why this is root-cause fix: `GatewayNodeSession.connect(...)` intentionally treats a changed `sessionBox.session` object identity as reconnect input, so reusing the same TLS session object while URL and TLS pin inputs are unchanged removes the false reconnect trigger at the macOS coordinator boundary without weakening the shared session replacement contract.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Healthy macOS node-mode direct `wss://` loops no longer supply a new TLS-pinning session identity on each pass, so unchanged connection inputs do not force `GatewayNodeSession` into reconnect state and repeat `mac node connected to gateway` notifications.
- Real environment tested: Source-level proof in the existing PR worktree on Linux; local macOS runtime proof was not available in this environment.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `daily_fix.sh run-validation --name diff-check-ci-lint-fix -- git diff --check`; `daily_fix.sh run-validation --name source-proof-ci-lint-fix -- python3 -c ...` source proof; attempted `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter MacNodeModeCoordinatorTests` before implementation.
- Evidence after fix: `diff-check-ci-lint-fix` passed; `source-proof-ci-lint-fix` passed and confirmed the coordinator now uses a lint-safe `Key: Equatable`, no longer has the 5-member tuple or SwiftFormat-triggering comment, still constructs the TLS-pinning session at one coordinator site, and still has reuse/rebuild regression tests. Terminal output copied from the after-fix source proof: `source proof passed: lint-safe TLS session cache key reuses cached session and tests cover reuse/rebuild`.
- Observed result after fix: The production path returns `self.tlsSessionCache.sessionBox(url: url, params: params)`, which keeps object identity stable for identical `GatewayTLSParams` and only rebuilds when the URL or TLS trust inputs change.
- What was not tested: `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter MacNodeModeCoordinatorTests` could not run because `swift` is not installed in this Linux environment; no live macOS direct `wss://` app session log proof was available.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/MacNodeModeCoordinatorTests.swift`.
- Scenario locked in: Repeated macOS direct `wss://` node-mode loop passes with the same URL and TLS params reuse one session object; changed TLS fingerprint input rebuilds it.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The regression lives at the macOS coordinator owner boundary before `GatewayNodeSession.connect(...)`, so these tests prove the exact identity-stability invariant without weakening shared gateway reconnect tests.
- Added `tls session cache reuses session box for unchanged params` to prove identical URL/TLS inputs return the same session object identity.
- Added `tls session cache rebuilds session box when params change` to prove fingerprint/TLS input changes still produce a new session identity and preserve intentional reconnects.
- Existing `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/GatewayNodeSessionTests.swift` still owns the shared contract that an explicitly changed session box rebuilds the gateway channel.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: Maintainer-decision / external runtime proof. The request asks for redacted macOS runtime logs or a recording; this Linux environment cannot run the macOS app coordinator path that triggers the direct `wss://` reconnect loop.
- RF-002: Maintainer-decision / proof gap disclosed. The PR body now keeps the source-level after-fix proof and explicitly states that live macOS runtime proof was not available locally.
- RF-003: Maintainer-decision / proof gap disclosed. No live macOS direct-gateway runtime proof is claimed from this environment; the available proof is the current-head coordinator source proof plus regression coverage.
- RF-004: Maintainer-decision. The code repair is narrow and complete; the remaining proof request is runtime evidence rather than a different automated code change.
- RF-005: Maintainer-decision / remote validation path. `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter MacNodeModeCoordinatorTests` still cannot run locally because `swift` is not installed here; the pushed `macos-swift` CI is the applicable Swift validation path after this lint repair.
- RF-006: Maintainer-decision / external runtime proof. The requested macOS direct `wss://` smoke test is not runnable from this Linux worktree, so this update does not claim that runtime log proof.

## Merge risk

- Why it matters / User impact: A healthy macOS node-mode direct gateway session should stay connected instead of silently self-reconnecting every few seconds, which reduces node churn, log noise, and secondary pairing/challenge instability.
- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk:behavior`, because this changes when macOS node mode rebuilds the TLS WebSocket session object.
- Risk explanation: The cache key includes URL, `required`, `expectedFingerprint`, `allowTOFU`, and `storeKey`, so real endpoint or TLS trust input changes still rebuild the session. Non-`wss://` paths invalidate the cache and keep returning nil.
- What did NOT change: Out of scope and unchanged are public API, config, schema, protocol, data format, token handling, pairing behavior, gateway reconnect policy, and non-macOS clients; this patch only changes macOS node-mode TLS session object reuse.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The source-of-truth boundary stays split correctly: `GatewayNodeSession` owns session-state reconnect semantics, while `MacNodeModeCoordinator` owns stable construction of macOS TLS-pinning session input for unchanged direct gateway configuration.
- Why acceptable: The change is scoped to macOS node-mode TLS session construction, keeps `GatewayNodeSession` identity semantics intact, and adds regression coverage for both reuse and rebuild behavior.
- Fix classification: Minimal canonical root-cause fix at the macOS coordinator owner boundary.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High confidence from source-level root cause, exact owner-boundary fix, and focused regression tests; proof gap is limited to local macOS runtime execution because this Linux environment lacks `swift`.
- Patch quality notes: No public API, config, schema, protocol, default behavior, compatibility path, or data format changes; no fallback behavior added. Related open PR scan during daily-fix initialization found no linked/competing open PR for #90668.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
